questions.question: Which among the following is an art of jewellery making?
questions.explanation: Thewa is a unique art of jewellery making which involves embossing of intricately worked-out sheet of gold. It originated in the state of Rajasthan. It derives from Tharna meaning hammer and Vada meaning silver wire.

questions.question: Alpana Folk art belong to which state?
questions.explanation: Alpana is a folk art of Bengal was mainly done on the floor and the walls of the house. The painting was done with hands (fingers are the brush) and the paint is mainly a paste comprising of rice flour. The motifs drawn are ritualistic images from mythology and scriptures. It is drawn by women of the house before the sunset. It is considered to ward off evil spirits and was specifically drawn on special occasions such as festivals or weddings.

questions.question: Which of the following is a tribal art form of Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: Jogi Art is tribal art form that hinges on lines and dots practiced in Rajasthan. It is mostly done in black and whites. It is practiced by artists from Magriwada area of Rajasthan.

questions.question: Which among the following is a miniature form of painting?
questions.explanation: Bundi Painting is a Rajasthani style of Indian miniature painting. It emphasizes on hunting, court scenes, procession, life of nobles, lovers, animals, birds and scenes from Lord Krishna's life. It is characterized by a fondness for lush green vegetation, dramatic night skies and a distinctive way of depicting water with light swirls.

questions.question: "Kirana Gharana" is associated with which of the following music forms?
questions.explanation: Kirana Gharana is one of the Hindustani Khayal gharanas. The central concern of the Kirana style is swara, precise tuning and expression of notes. Ustad Abdul Karim Khan and Ustad Abdul Waheed Khan established this gharana.

questions.question: Vesak Poya festival is related to which of the following religion?
questions.explanation: Vesak poya is also known as Buddha Purnima or Buddha Day. It commemorates the birth, enlightenment (nirvana) and death (parinirvana) of Gautam Buddha in the Theravad or southern tradition. The name of the observance represents a month in the Hindu calendar falling in April-May i.e. Vaishaka. On this day the followers gather and sing hymns in praise of the holy triple gem: The Buddha, The Dharma (his teachings), and The Sangha (his disciples).

questions.question: "Ka Bom" is a traditional drum of which among the following state?
questions.explanation: It is a traditional drum of Khasi community in Meghalaya. The Khasi people are an indigenous tribe, the majority of whom live in the State of Meghalaya which is in the north-eastern part of India. The Khasi people of the War sub-tribe designed and built the famous living root bridges of the Cherrapunjee region.

questions.question: Kaniyan Koothu is performed in which state?
questions.explanation: Kaniyan koothu is a ritual art form practiced during temple festivals in Tamil Nadu. It is performed only by men. It is performed in weddings, deaths and functions at homes. It is based on Puranic stories such as Markandeya and Harischandra Puranam and tales from Ramayana and Mahabarata, besides local dieties.

questions.question: Phulkari craft belongs to which state?
questions.explanation: It is a form of craft in which embroidery is done in a simple and sparse design over shawls and dupattas. It is practised in Punjab. The threads used were of a silk yarn called pat. Phulkari and baghs are worn by women across Punjab during marriages, festivals and other joyous occasions.

questions.question: Which among the following folk dance is performed by two religious communities jointly?
questions.explanation: Jagor is a dance drama of Goa based on no continuous plot or narrative. It is performed jointly by the Hindus and Christians, to offer prayer to deity to protect the village from the river water. It is faith that it averts natural calamities and quarrels among themselves.

questions.question: Which among the following is the first work of Sir Arthur Canon Doyle to be published?
options: The Adventure of the Empty House
questions.explanation: In 1887 Sir Arthur Conan Doyle published A Study in Scarlet, the first of four novels about Holmes and Dr. Watson. The Sherlock Holmes stories are generally considered milestones in the field of crime fiction. Doyle is also known for writing the fictional adventures of Professor Challenger and for propagating the mystery of the Mary Celeste.

questions.question: Which among the following is the work of Sidney Sheldon?
questions.explanation: The Naked Face is the first novel (1970) written by Sidney Sheldon. It was nominated by the Mystery Writers of America for the Edgar Allan Poe Award for Best First Novel by an American Author. Sidney Sheldon was an American writer and producer.

questions.question: Which among the following is the autobiography of former US President Barack Obama?
questions.explanation: Dreams from My Father: A Story of Race and Inheritance is a memoir by Barack Obama, who was elected as U.S. President in 2008. It explores events of his early years up until his entry into law school in 1988.

questions.question: Which among the following is an autobiography of an Indian sports person?
questions.explanation: A Shot at History: My Obsessive Journey to Olympic Gold is 2011 autobiography of Indian Olympic Gold medalist Abhinav Bindra. He won the medal in the 10-metre air rifle category, which made him India's first ever individual gold medalist.

questions.question: Who co-authored the book autobiography of Sachin Tendulkar's Playing it my way?
questions.explanation: Playing It My Way is the autobiography of former Indian cricketer Sachin Tendulkar. It was co-authored by Boria Majumdar. Boria Majumdar is an Indian sports journalist, academician and author.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book "Dramatic Decade: The Indira Gandhi Years"?
questions.explanation: The book was authored by former President of India, Pranab Mukerjee. It deals with several important things that defined India during 1969-80 - the consequences it had on our economy as more than 10 million refugees had to be taken care of by a government strapped of cash and foodgrains and the turmoil it caused in Bengal Congress politics, in the runup to the war with Pakistan.

questions.question: Who among the following edited the book "Letters for a nation" of Jawaharlal Nehru?
questions.explanation: The book was edited by Madhav Khosla. It contains the letters penned by Jawaharlal Nehru to his Chief Ministers, between 1947 and 1963. This collection covers a range of themes and subjects, including citizenship, war and peace, law and order, national planning and development, governance and corruption, and India's place in the world. The letters also cover momentous world events and the many crises and conflicts the country faced during the first sixteen years after Independence.

questions.question: A House for Mr. Biswas was the debut novel of who among the following?
questions.explanation: A House for Mr Biswas is a 1961 debut novel by V. S. Naipaul. Time magazine included the novel in its "TIME 100 Best English-language Novels from 1923 to 2005". V. S. Naipaul, is a Nobel Prize-winning British writer who was born in Trinidad.

questions.question: Which of the following book was authored by Jaswant Singh?
questions.explanation: India at Risk was authored by Jaswant Singh. It gives an analysis of the major challenges that India has faced over the last sixty-six years: The conflicts, the issues and the consequences that remain with us today.

questions.question: The book "Two Saints" talks about Ramakrishna Paramahamsa and who among the following saints?
questions.explanation: The book was authored by Arun Shourie. It talks about speculations around and about Ramakrishna Paramahamsa and Ramana Maharishi. Paramahamsa and Maharshi have been among the greatest spiritual figures of India.

questions.question: Who was the first recipient of Param Vir Chakra?
questions.explanation: Major Somnath Sharma, an Indian Army officer, was the first recipient of the Param Vir Chakra. He was posthumously awarded the Param Vir Chakra for his actions in Indo-Pakistan War of 1947. Param Vir Chakra is India's highest military decoration awarded for the displaying distinguished acts of valor during wartime. It is awarded to officers or enlisted personnel from all branches of the Indian military.

questions.question: Indira Gandhi Peace Prize was first awarded in which year?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi Peace Prize was first awarded in the year 1986. It is a prestigious award accorded annually by India to individuals or organizations in recognition of creative efforts toward promoting international peace, development and a new international economic order. It is also referred to as Indira Gandhi Prize for Peace, Disarmament and Development.

questions.question: Who was the first recipient of Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna award?
questions.explanation: In the year 1991-92, Vishwanath Anand was conferred the award. He is also country's first chess Grandmaster and a five-time winner of World Chess Championship. This award is the highest sporting honor in India. It is awarded annually by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.

questions.question: Of the following awards which is NOT conferred on children?
questions.explanation: Sarvottam Jeevan Raksha Padak is awarded to civilians to reward saving lives from drowning, fire, or mine accidents. Other than this, all the above-mentioned awards along with National Bravery Award are given to children who were between the ages of six to eighteen when they committed their acts of bravery. The awards are given by the Government of India and the Indian Council for Child Welfare. It was instituted in 1957.

questions.question: Fields Medal is associated with which among the following field?
questions.explanation: Fields Medal is associated with Mathematics. It is the highest scientific award for mathematicians, and is presented every four years at the International Congress of Mathematicians. The awards recognize both existing work and the promise of future achievement, presented to mathematicians not over forty at the year of the Congress.

questions.question: Which among the following award is regarded as "Nobel Prize in Mathematics"?
questions.explanation: Fields Medal is referred to as Nobel Prize in Mathematics. The award is officially known as "International medals for outstanding discoveries in mathematics". It is named in honor of Professor J. C. Fields, a Canadian mathematician. Medal is administered by International Mathematical Union. It is an international non-governmental and non-profit scientific organization, with the purpose of promoting international cooperation in mathematics.

questions.question: Which country presents "Great Immigrants Award" to its citizens?
questions.explanation: Great Immigrants Awards are presented to naturalized citizens of USA. It is administered by Carnegie Corporation and the award is annually presented on US's Independence Day, July 4ᵗʰ. It is awarded in recognition of their contribution to USA's economy, culture and society.

questions.question: Which among the following country hosts Asian Awards Ceremony?
questions.explanation: Asian awards are hosted by United Kingdom. It recognizes and rewards exemplary achievement across 14 categories that include business, philanthropy, entertainment, culture and sport. The Asian Awards were opened to all people of both South and Eastern Asian origin.

questions.question: Rajiv Gandhi National Quality Awards are sponsored by which among the following institution?
options: Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce & Industry
Associated Chambers of Commerce of India
questions.explanation: The Rajiv Gandhi National Quality Award is the national quality award given by the Bureau of Indian Standards to Indian organisations that show excellence in their performance. The award aims to promote quality services to the consumers and to give special recognition to organisations that contribute significantly towards the quality movement of India. It is awarded to organizations from any of 5 different categories.

questions.question: Which among the following award is presented by Ministry of Home Affairs?
questions.explanation: All the above-mentioned awards are presented by Ministry of Home Affairs. Along with this the ministry also presents Police Medals, Jeevan Raksha Padak, Kabir Purashkar, Communal harmony Awards, Fire Service Medals and Home Guard & Civil Defence awards.
